architecture_fit.note: Minimal attention-only 2L/4H delta from base_model.py with a real CUDA forward; counts correctly but is somewhat circular since the model is hand-built to contain exactly 2 induction heads rather than an independently trained checkpoint.

baseline_comparison.note: Two strawmen under the same measurement (none-wired -> count 0, all-eight-wired -> count 8) bracket the result and show the metric discriminates, plus the always-guess-4 benchmark baseline.

faithfulness.note: Real causal ablation on the model's own logits: knocking out either induction head collapses copy accuracy to chance (0.016) while removing all six distractors leaves it at 1.0.

operating_range.note: Threshold sweep shows count stays at 2 across 0.05-1.0 (AUC 0.975), but separation is degenerate (1.0 vs 0.016) and there is no variation across delay/length/noise scale.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Explicit hand-set QK/OV weights expressed as torch tensors on CUDA reproduce the target counting behaviour exactly — textbook hardcoded-circuit demonstration.

visual_judgement.note: Demo tab leads with the per-head bar chart + 0.5 count line (count = bars above line) and a causal-ablation bar chart, making both the claim and its faithfulness legible at a glance.

visualisation_rationale.note: README's 'Why this visualisation' cleanly ties the bar chart to the count, the ablation chart to faithfulness, and the strawman summary to the metric's discriminating power.

notes: A well-executed hand-built circuit: real GPU forward pass, hardcoded QK/OV weights, and a genuine causal-ablation faithfulness check that knocks out each counted head and watches the copy task break. Main limitation is circularity (the model is constructed to contain exactly 2 induction heads rather than discovered in a trained checkpoint) and a degenerate score separation that leaves operating-range robustness untested across input scale. Mean of scored rubric items ~4.4 -> good.
